macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low Amid War with Iran and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment dropped to a record low of 49.8 in April, the weakest since 1978, due to war with Iran, oil disruptions, and rising inflation expectations to 4.7% over the next year. Brent crude traded near $105 per barrel, up 17% weekly, amid a 57% decline in Gulf oil output.

items.title: Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Confirmation Highly Likely
items.summary: Moody's economists discussed the week's economic news, highlighting the now highly likely confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair nominee.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Nears $80K Amid US-Iran Ceasefire Extension
items.summary: Bitcoin surged above $78,000 after the US-Iran ceasefire extension announcement, reaching near $80K before retracing, with over $500 million in liquidations in 24 hours. The market saw $189 million in total liquidations, mostly longs.

items.title: DYDX Surges 18% as Supply Shrinks and Volume Explodes
items.summary: DYDX rallied 18% in the past 24 hours due to shrinking supply and exploding trading volume, with price battling at $0.16 resistance.

items.title: Shiba Inu Burn Rate Jumps 700% with 23.62M Tokens Burned
items.summary: Shiba Inu saw a 700% surge in burn rate over 24 hours, burning 23.62 million SHIB tokens worth $141, amid high trading activity and optimism shown by more short liquidations.

items.title: Crypto Market Liquidations Hit $189 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: The crypto market experienced $189 million in liquidations over 24 hours, with Bitcoin longs at $30.45 million and Ethereum longs heavily impacted.

items.title: Kalshi Bans Three US Candidates Over Political Insider Trading
items.summary: Prediction market platform Kalshi cracked down on political insider trading by banning three US candidates.

items.title: Peter Schiff Labels Strategy’s STRC a Ponzi Scheme
items.summary: Economist Peter Schiff criticized Strategy’s STRC initiative as a Ponzi scheme.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro, Outperforming Claude Opus 4.7 in Benchmarks
items.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.5, codenamed 'Spud', as its smartest and most intuitive model yet, surpassing Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 in major benchmarks. The release follows leaks and a cryptic X post, marking a significant upgrade over GPT-5.4.

items.title: Anthropic's Opus 4.7 Claims 'Taste' in AI Capabilities for Professional Tasks
items.summary: Anthropic's latest model, Opus 4.7, is described as more tasteful and creative, producing higher-quality interfaces, slides, and documents. This highlights debates on whether AI can achieve uniquely human traits like taste.

items.title: Delivery Hero Unveils Herogen, AI Agent Equivalent to 130 Senior Engineers
items.summary: Delivery Hero launched Herogen, an autonomous AI software delivery agent built on LLMs, delivering coding output equal to 130 senior engineers annually with expanding capacity and built-in security features.

items.title: Orkes Raises $60M for AI Workflow Orchestration Platform
items.summary: Orkes secured $60 million in Series B funding to support developers deploying AI applications in production, built by Netflix's original microservices architects.

items.title: Hippocratic AI Partners with Health Systems for Generative AI Voice Agents
items.summary: Hippocratic AI expanded partnerships with leading health systems to deploy safe generative AI voice agents aimed at improving patient outcomes.

items.title: Meta Plans 8,000 Layoffs Amid Rising AI Infrastructure Costs
items.summary: Meta is set to lay off nearly 8,000 employees next month to offset booming AI spending, while Microsoft offers voluntary retirements amid tech reductions.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Tomodachi Life Achieves Huge Opening Sales in Japan
items.summary: Famitsu sales charts show Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream with a massive launch, expected to reach 10 million sales. Nintendo is celebrating with in-game rewards for players.

items.title: Microsoft Gaming Reverts to Xbox Branding
items.summary: Microsoft Gaming is reverting back to Xbox branding amid other changes like Game Pass price adjustments and reevaluation of exclusivity strategies.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Officially Revealed
items.summary: A resynced version of Assassin's Creed Black Flag has been officially announced, generating excitement among fans.

items.title: Duke Nukem 3D Ray Tracing Version Released
items.summary: Developer Post Memetic released Duke Nukem 3D RT, a new ray-traced version of the classic game, highlighting the resurgence of retro gaming mods.

items.title: GameCube and Wii Games Now Running on Modded Nintendo Switch
items.summary: The homebrew community has enabled GameCube and Wii games to run on modded Nintendo Switch consoles.

items.title: Google Executive Reveals Game Studios Using AI Secretly
items.summary: A Google executive stated that game studios are secretly incorporating AI into their development processes.

items.title: PSN Rolls Out Age Verification in UK and Ireland
items.summary: PlayStation Network is implementing age verification in the UK and Ireland, requiring users to submit personal data amid ongoing PS Store issues.

items.title: Nagoshi Studios Deletes YouTube Channel
items.summary: Nagoshi Studios, known for Yakuza and Judgment series, has deleted their entire YouTube channel.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to Step Down, John Ternus to Take Over in September
items.summary: After over 15 years, Tim Cook is handing over Apple CEO role to Senior VP of Hardware Engineering John Ternus in September. Ternus led engineering for iPhone, iPad, Mac, and AirPods, key in Apple Silicon transition.[1]

items.title: Meta Announces Layoffs of 8,000 Employees Amid AI Spending Growth
items.summary: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs, approximately 10% of its staff, as part of cost reductions while increasing AI investments.[2][3]

items.title: Apple Patches iOS Flaw Exploited by FBI to Access Deleted Signal Messages
items.summary: Latest iPhone update fixes a bug that allowed FBI to recover deleted Signal notifications in a forensic case.[2]

items.title: Tesla Begins Robotaxi Production with Cybercab Ramp-Up Expected by Year-End
items.summary: Tesla has started production of its robotaxi, with the Cybercab expected to accelerate manufacturing by the end of the year.[4]

items.title: Motorola Razr 70 Ultra Leaks with Full Camera Specs and Renders
items.summary: New leaks reveal Motorola's Razr 70 Ultra (also called Razr Ultra) with detailed camera gallery and official-looking renders.[2]

items.title: Honor 600 and 600 Pro Launch in Europe with Pricing Details
items.summary: Honor has released the 600 and 600 Pro smartphones in Europe, including full pricing information.[2]

items.title: Germany Launches Spying Probe into Signal Attacks Targeting MPs
items.summary: German authorities have initiated an investigation into Signal messaging attacks aimed at members of parliament.[4]
